column2: Identify the highlighted modular connector used for Ethernet twisted-pair cabling.
column6:
Exam Trap

RJ45 and RJ11 are both modular connectors, but RJ45 is the larger Ethernet connector in this objective. RJ11 is associated with telephone or DSL service.
column9: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes

column2: Identify the highlighted fiber connector that uses a twist-to-lock style.
column6:
Why

ST is associated with a twist-to-lock style. SC is a push-pull connector, and LC is a smaller clip-style connector.
column9: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes
column10: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.29; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.2
column11: A+::220-1201::3.2 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::CableTypesConnectorsFeaturesandPurposes Image HighYield FiberConnectors Connectors Source::Messer-v170

column2: Identify this USB connector commonly used on host ports and many peripheral cables.
column6:
Exam Trap

USB-A is the familiar flat rectangular USB connector. Do not confuse the connector shape with the USB signaling generation, such as USB 2.0 or USB 3.0.
column9: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes
column10: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.25; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.2; Wikimedia Commons image metadata, docs/image-sources/3.2-cable-types-connectors-features-and-purposes.md
column11: A+::220-1201::3.2 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::CableTypesConnectorsFeaturesandPurposes Image USB Connectors Source::Messer-v170

column2: Identify this USB connector with the squarer peripheral-side shape.
column6:
Why

USB-B is often recognized by its squarer body compared with USB-A. It is a connector shape, not a separate USB speed by itself.
column9: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes

column2: Identify this older small USB connector that is larger than MicroUSB.
column6:
Exam Trap

MiniUSB and MicroUSB are both older small USB connector families. MiniUSB is larger and thicker than MicroUSB.
column9: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes
column10: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.25; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.2; Wikimedia Commons image metadata, docs/image-sources/3.2-cable-types-connectors-features-and-purposes.md
column11: A+::220-1201::3.2 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::CableTypesConnectorsFeaturesandPurposes Image MiniUSB USB Connectors Source::Messer-v170

column2: Identify this thin older USB connector commonly seen on small devices before USB-C became common.
column6:
Exam Trap

MicroUSB is thinner than MiniUSB and is not reversible like USB-C.
column9: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes
column10: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.25; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.2; Wikimedia Commons image metadata, docs/image-sources/3.2-cable-types-connectors-features-and-purposes.md
column11: A+::220-1201::3.2 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::CableTypesConnectorsFeaturesandPurposes Image MicroUSB USB Connectors Source::Messer-v170

column6:
Exam Trap

USB-C is the connector shape. It can carry different signals depending on device and cable support, so the connector alone does not guarantee a specific speed or feature.
